LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Interaction.label: social interaction
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Interaction.prompt: Write a direct social interaction between pawns from the POV pawn's side of the moment.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Interaction.enhancement: Make the exchange feel live in the room: a look, pause, flinch, heat, or silence. Keep it tied to the supplied social text; do not add unstated history or dialogue.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MentalState.label: mental state
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MentalState.prompt: Write a mental-state event as immediate pressure on thought, impulse, and action.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MentalState.enhancement: Write with pressure and unstable momentum: thought, impulse, body, then action. Stay inside the named state and observed behavior; do not diagnose beyond supplied facts.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Tale.label: tale
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Tale.prompt: Write a notable non-social RimWorld tale as something the pawn lived through or remembers.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Tale.enhancement: Find the dramatic pressure in the tale facts: danger, pride, shame, relief, damage, or aftermath. Do not turn the entry into ordinary social chatter.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MoodEvent.label: mood event
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MoodEvent.prompt: Write a colony-wide mood event through how it touches this pawn.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MoodEvent.enhancement: Show the condition pressing on the pawn's senses or nerves rather than explaining game mechanics; keep the condition's scope clear.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Thought.label: thought
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Thought.prompt: Write a temporary thought or mood memory as inner pressure, relief, or irritation.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Thought.enhancement: Make the thought bite, comfort, itch, or linger in the pawn's mind. Treat it as internal context, not as a new external event unless the facts say so.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Inspiration.label: inspiration
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Inspiration.prompt: Write inspiration as sudden useful clarity catching in the pawn's mind.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Inspiration.enhancement: Make the clarity feel electric and actionable: a sudden plan, urge, or confidence. Focus on what the pawn wants to do next; do not invent success.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Romance.label: romance
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Romance.prompt: Write a relationship milestone as an emotional change between the two people.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Romance.enhancement: Make the changed bond feel risky and personal: hope, embarrassment, ache, relief, or dread. Do not backfill a conversation or private scene.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Work.label: work
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Work.prompt: Write sampled colony work as a concrete moment in the pawn's labor.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Work.enhancement: Use tactile work detail: weight, heat, dirt, rhythm, tools, mistakes, or satisfaction. Let passion, strain, or dread come only from supplied fields.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Hediff.label: health condition
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Hediff.prompt: Write a health-condition event through the pawn's body and mood.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Hediff.enhancement: Make the body unavoidable: pain, weakness, breath, fever, hunger, balance, or fear. Do not invent treatment, diagnosis, or recovery.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Raid.label: raid
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Raid.prompt: Write a raid or hostile arrival from the pawn's immediate experience of danger.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Raid.enhancement: Write close to the body: noise, cover, orders, fear, pain, smoke, blood, or the scramble to survive. Keep every detail grounded; do not invent battle outcomes.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Quest.label: quest
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Quest.prompt: Write a quest lifecycle event as promise, resolution, or loss.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Quest.enhancement: Make the promise feel personal: risk, duty, hope, doubt, cost, relief, or regret. Use the lifecycle signal and supplied quest facts; do not invent rewards, blame, or consequences.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ritual.label: ritual
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ritual.prompt: Write a finished Ideology ritual from the pawn's assigned place in it.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ritual.enhancement: Use the supplied ritual role, ritual title, behavior, quality, and pawn status fields. Let quality shape confidence, aftermath, and emotional weight, but do not name or explain the quality label directly. Keep the entry grounded in what completion would feel like from that role; do not invent ritual effects beyond the supplied outcome.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ability.label: ability
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ability.prompt: Write a successful pawn ability use as a concrete moment of focus, risk, or exertion.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Ability.enhancement: Use the supplied ability name, category, target, and cooldown facts. Treat short-cooldown abilities as quick minor moments and long-cooldown abilities as rarer, weightier choices; do not invent extra effects.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_DayReflection.label: day reflection
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_DayReflection.prompt: Write an end-of-day reflection that weighs the day as a whole.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_DayReflection.enhanceme: Choose the supplied moments that still sting, glow, or weigh on the pawn tonight instead of listing every event.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Arrival.label: arrival
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Arrival.prompt: Write a factual colony-arrival note about how this pawn joined or began here.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Arrival.enhancement: Make the arrival feel like a threshold: dust, shock, silence, relief, suspicion, or first sight of shelter. Use only scenario and arrival facts.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Death.label: death
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Death.prompt: Write a factual death note about how the colonist died.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Death.enhancement: Make the death note stark and specific, with one concrete cause or setting detail. Use only supplied cause, body-part, weapon, condition, and setting facts; keep it brief.
